D1 Optics L2 Understand Reference

Twilight, the Blue Hour & Earth's Shadow

sink the sun below the horizon and watch civil, nautical, and astronomical twilight pass

After sunset the sky stays lit by sunlight scattered from ever-higher air. Three phases mark the descent, and Earth's shadow climbs the eastern sky.

Lower the sun below the horizon to step through the phases. Pan the view from the sunset glow toward the anti-solar sky to find Earth's blue-grey shadow capped by the pink Belt of Venus.
